title: Review: feat(android): foreground service keeps relay alive when backgrounded
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — auth-failure stop gap is the only real correctness issue; everything else is polish. The core lifecycle (start on auth success, stop on user disconnect) is correct.
Confidence: 85% chance of no missed critical bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) no test coverage for Android lifecycle visible in diff; (2) did not run the app or test on a real device; (3) could not see whether any existing test exercises the auth-failure path in relay_service; (4) Android foreground service behaviour on specific OEM skins (Samsung, Xiaomi) not verifiable statically. Would raise confidence if: test exercising auth failure → disconnect() flow existed, or if I could grep relay_service_test to confirm the _authFailed path is covered.
